domain_experience: no clear niche domain advantage provided
distribution: must rely on marketplaces, communities, or search-driven discovery
unfair_advantages: but weak edge versus a random competent developer with ChatGPT

opportunities.title: Marketplace-sold micro-tool packs for a narrow workflow
opportunities.thesis: Sell small AI-assisted utilities in channels where buyers already exist instead of trying to create demand from a blank start.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can build and package useful mini-tools cheaply, but only has a modest edge because distribution and domain specialization are weak.
validation_plan: Find buyer pools that already purchase low-ticket tools.
Pick one workflow complaint repeated in reviews or communities.
Publish a landing page or listing and test for signups or purchases before expanding scope.
kill_criteria: No repeated pain point found in public
No interest from targeted distribution channels
Too many near-identical competitors with stronger positioning

opportunities.title: Resume, job-search, or freelance proposal optimizer sold self-serve
opportunities.thesis: Ship quickly in a familiar consumer problem space, but accept that the market is crowded and weakly differentiated.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can build fast, but has almost no founder edge or distribution advantage here.
validation_plan: Test niche-specific messaging against generic job-search tooling.
Only proceed if the niche is painfully specific and shows paid intent quickly.
tool_specs: Templates tuned to one user segment
kill_criteria: No reason to pick it over existing free tools

opportunities.title: Micro-SaaS for a narrow operational workflow discovered from public communities
opportunities.thesis: Observe repeated complaints in public communities, then build the smallest narrow workflow tool around one real pain point.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user's edge is only speed of implementation; without niche access, discovery risk remains high.
validation_plan: Collect repeated workflow complaints from one buyer segment.
Test a pre-sell page before building.
Ship a one-job MVP only if buyers attempt to pay.
tool_specs: Tiny SaaS with one core workflow
kill_criteria: Problem requires broad platform or heavy integrations

opportunities.title: Simple data or alerting product for public information
opportunities.thesis: Combine public data and AI summarization into a focused alert or dashboard product for one small buyer segment.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can assemble the product cheaply, but lacks proprietary data and direct buyer access.
validation_plan: Test whether buyers want summarized alerts rather than raw data.
Verify public data access and policy safety before building.
kill_criteria: Source data is unreliable or risky
Users do not value the summary enough to pay

opportunities.title: Directory-first wedge into a self-serve utility product
opportunities.thesis: Start with a searchable, useful asset for a narrow selection problem, monetize access or adjacent tooling, and productize only after observing real usage.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This path reduces the need for calls and services, leverages shipping ability, and uses existing buyer intent better than a blank-slate micro-SaaS.
validation_plan: Choose a buyer pool with built-in distribution.
List repeated complaints and selection pain points.
Launch a sharp landing page with a concrete promise and price.
Build the smallest working version only if buyers engage or attempt purchase within a week.
tool_specs: Single-purpose web app or searchable directory
kill_criteria: No buyer pool with built-in distribution found within 3 days
Pain point is not repeated publicly
Landing page gets no meaningful interest
Product still feels like a generic AI wrapper

opportunities.title: No-call concierge onboarding into a narrow automation product
opportunities.thesis: Offer an asynchronous fixed-scope setup for one repetitive workflow, then convert delivery steps into software.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can avoid live calls and learn quickly, but this still conflicts with the stated desire to avoid services.
validation_plan: Test a fixed-price async offer with no meetings.
Document each manual step for later productization.

next_actions: Stop pursuing a generic AI micro-SaaS thesis.
Choose three buyer pools with built-in distribution.
Extract ten repeated pain points from public complaints.
Launch one sharp landing page with pricing within 72 hours.
Only build the smallest working product if real interest appears.
Kill weak ideas fast and repeat validation instead of polishing.
